LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, CONTRIBUTION, BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT, REFORMATION AND DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE THE "RESOLUTION OF CONTRACT DISPUTES" PROVISION CANNOT REASONABLY BE READ TO REQUIRE DGS TO RESOLVE THE DISPUTE THAT CYPRESS HAD/HAS WITH ITS EMPLOYEES. PER GOVERNMENT CODE 815 AND MIKLOSY V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 876, 899, CYPRESS' CLAIMS MUST BE BASED ON A STATUTE AND THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS A COMMON LAW CLAIM WITHOUT A STATUTORY BASIS. THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION ARE FAIRLY GROUNDED IN THE PARTIES' CONTRACT AND THUS ARE PERMITTED BY GOVERNMENT CODE 814. THE BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT AND REFORMATION CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. THERE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER THE PARTIES HAD A MEETING OF THE MINDS REGARDING THE MEANING OF SECTION 19134 WHEN THEY ENTERED INTO THE CONTRACT. THE FACE OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT NECESSARILY OR CONCLUSIVELY SHOW THAT ANY OF THE GOVERNMENTAL IMMUNITIES BAR ANY OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the first and sixth through tenth causes of action for unpaid wages and benefits, breach of contract, breach of implied covenant of good faith, constructive fraud, intentional and negligent misrepresentation in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiffs is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs are intended third-party beneficiaries to the contracts entered between Cypress and Department of General Services. Gov't Code 19134 requires fair compensation and benefits for individuals such as the plaintiffs. Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts for each causes of action at issue. Cypress is inappropriately seeking to have the Court reach the merits of the case at the pleadings stage.

Counsel for the Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and also complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840, mlyon@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gary S. Kletter of the law offices of Kletter & Nguyen Law LLP (650-577-2336) appears as counsel for plaintiff/non-opposing party

Attorney, Jaemin Chang of the law offices of Fox Rothschild LLP (415-364-5540) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/partially-opposing party, Cypress Security, LLC

Deputy Attorney General, John T. McGlothlin of the California Department of Justice (510-879-0295) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party, California Department of General Services

Attorney, David D. King, Labor Relations Counsel of the Calidornia Department of Human Resources (916-324-0512) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party, California Department of Human Resources

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, the Court first confirms the tentative ruling as posted. Then, during argument of co-defendant, Department of General Services' demurrer, the Court modifies and re-confirms the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT filed by Cypress Security, LLC is sustained without leave to amend as to first, second, fifth and seventh causes of action for equitable indemnity, contribution, and negligent misrepresentation. Per Government Code 815 and Miklosy v. Regents of University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 899, Cypress' claims must be based on a statute and causes of action one, two, and five are common law claims without a statutory basis. Government Code 814 is inapplicable since Cypress did not have a contractual relationship with DHR. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gary S. Kletter of the law offices of Kletter & Nguyen Law LLP (650-577-2336) appears as counsel for plaintiff/non-opposing party

Attorney, Jaemin Chang of the law offices of Fox Rothschild LLP (415-364-5540) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/partially-opposing party, Cypress Security, LLC

Deputy Attorney General, John T. McGlothlin of the California Department of Justice (510-879-0295) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party, California Department of General Services

Attorney, David D. King, Labor Relations Counsel of the Calidornia Department of Human Resources (916-324-0512) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/demurring party, California Department of Human Resources

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed with modification as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT filed by Cypress Security, LLC is sustained without leave to amend as to the third, fifth and seventh causes of action for breach of contract and negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to the first, second, fourth, and sixth causes of action for equitable indemnity, contribution, breach of the implied covenant, reformation and declaratory relief. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The breach of contract claim fails because the "Resolution of Contract Disputes" provision cannot reasonably be read to require DGS to resolve the dispute that Cypress had/has with its employees. Per Government Code 815 and Miklosy v. Regents of University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 899, Cypress' claims must be based on a statute and the fifth cause of action is a common law claim without a statutory basis. The first and second causes of action are fairly grounded in the parties' contract and thus are permitted by Government Code 814. The breach of implied covenant and reformation claims are adequately pled. There is a factual issue whether the parties had a meeting of the minds regarding the meaning of section 19134 when they entered into the contract. The face of the cross-complaint does not necessarily or conclusively show that any of the governmental immunities bar any of the claims alleged in the cross-complaint. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS' Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED. California's policy favoring the liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings and the lack of any discernable prejudice strongly supports the granting of the motion. Enlargement of the claims and issues in a case and addition of complexity to a case are not the kind of prejudice to a defendant that warrants denying a motion for leave to amend. If defendant Cypress Security, LLC believes that any of the new claims are inadequately or defectively pled, its remedy is to file a demurrer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
